text.p: SECTION A: AFRICAN DRAMA

1) It was is believed in Yoruba land that no Yoruba should fight other Yoruba nor capture another Yoruba person as slave just as was testified by Lawumi during her conversation with Anlugbua. But the people of Owu did not listen to this adivce rather, they went fought Ife people their ancestoral town, capturing their women and taking them as slaves and looting their stalls at Apomu market.

The Owu people fighting their ancestoral town what which was forbidden in Yoruba land led to the capturing In Iyunloye the wife of Okunade as one of the women captures. This woman capture " Iyunloye" in caused great bitterness in her husband Okunade that he abandoned his profession as an artist to join the army and rose to the post of a Mayor who led the Allied forces to destroy Owu land.
SECTION B: Non-African Drama
Bernard Shaw: Arms and the Man
6) The Use of humor in the play
Humor is anything that envokes laughter in the audience in a literary work.
Humor is seen in Arms and the Man, when the fugitive in the girl's (Raina's) room chambar revealed that his gun is loaded with cream chocoalate instead of bullets.
There is also humor about the disapp-earance 3 characters of the Raina's portrait in the pocke pocket of the coat. (Petk

2 characters Wh General Major general Petkoff saw the potrait in the coat's pocket initially, but when he wanted to ask Raina how her potrait managed to enter his pocket, he dipped his hand into the pocket only to find out that the potrait is no longer there.
There is also humor about the ent enterince of Nicola with Bluntschli's loggage.
Section C: African Poetry
The Boy on the Swing
Simplicity of the "Boy on the swing" as deceptive
ASARE KONADU - A Woman in her Prime
3) The superstitions of Brenhoma people
The people of Brenhoma believed that children are gifts from the gods.

This believe can be seen from womens visiting their gods like tano Tano. for Adwoa Pokuwa for example, visited Tano on several occassion because of her barreness and was given some herbs and told to carry out prescribed sacrifices to the gods which she did.

There is also superstition that the dead lives in another world and that their spirit can come out to do good or bad. This belie- 2 words ve made the Brenhoma people to worship their ancestors. for instance, when the fire caused by thunderstorm struck the tree at the centre of the vill-age, the people believed that it was caused by their an angry spirit of their dead fathers (ancestors) and so they preppared a feast to appease the spirits and to their land.
SECTION B: Non-African Prose
ERNEST HEMMINGWAY: The Old Man and the Sea
The relationship between Santiago and the boy and the significance of the bond.
Santiago is the old experienced fisherman who went fishing for eighty-four day but came back without any fish.
The boy is young Monalin is a young boy whom Santiago had taught how to fish right from when he was five years old.
The boy (Monalin) helped Santiago in many was: he helped him by carring his fishing tools as seen in page 5 and even provided food, coffee and beer for him (the old man).
The relationship between the Santiago and the boy is caudial. ) They discused on various topics of interest to them like sports fishing etc. like father and child.
Santiago lovesd the boy and the boy also loves him as seen by the boy's pity for the old man for his misfortune/unlucky.
The way 1 character Santiago loves the boy shows the way elderly ones should other younger ones who may not be the own children and younger ones should help love enderly ones and help them.
